observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

   1. For  the  Municipal  Mayor,  to  assign  additional  manpower  to  the
      Accounting Office whose main function is to focus on  the  preparation
      of the 17 Bank  Reconciliation  Statements  in  order  to  update  and
      prioritize its timely submission.

   2. For the Municipal Accountant, effect the necessary  adjusting  journal
      entries once the supporting documents are already complete in order to
      establish the validity and accuracy of the  Cash-in-Bank  balances  in
      the financial statements.

Recommendations:

We  recommended  the  enforcement  of  the  immediate  liquidation  of   the
outstanding cash  advances  thru  the  issuance  of  letters  of  demand  to
officials and employees concerned and strict compliance with the  provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and  liquidation  of
cash advances.


Also, continue the conduct of verification and analysis of the existence  of
receivables and unliquidated  cash  advances  to  determine  the  extent  of
collectability. A request  for  the  write-off  shall  be  made  on  dormant
receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the LGU shall take advantage of  the  latest  technology
by adopting the system  of  payroll  payment  through  an  automated  teller
machine of Development Bank of the Philippines  (DBP)  -  Mati  City,  Davao
Oriental, its depository bank,  in  order  to  strengthen  control  of  cash
management.

In addition, the management was encouraged to follow up with its  depository
bank on the status of their request in order to  expedite  the  installation
of ATM  whether  all  requirements  for  the  said  installation  have  been
complied with.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

     1. For the Municipal Accountant, prepare  and  maintain  the  property
        ledger cards of the purchase property and equipment in CY 2021  and
        ultimately all the corresponding PPE ledger cards for all  recorded
        PPE in the books.

     2. For the GSO,  prepare  and  maintain  the  property  cards  of  the
        identified property and equipment that were acquired in CY 2021 and
        all PPE Cards of the PPE owned by the LGU.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1. Direct the Chairperson of the said task force to exert all possible
        efforts in locating and collecting the missing or lacking documents
        necessary to transfer the title of the aforesaid parcels of land in
        the name of the LGU in order  not  to  incur  a  further  delay  in
        establishing the ownership of the Municipality.


     2. Allocate sufficient funds for the payment of processing  fees  that
        may be incurred in the titling thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

     1. Create an Inventory Committee who shall be temporarily relieved  of
        all their regular duties to devote their full time to  the  conduct
        of the physical inventory taking until the  same  is  completed  in
        three (3) months or less as required under Sections 5.2 and 5.3  of
        the said COA Circular.


     2. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan
        (PIP), have it approved by the  Municipal  Mayor,  and  submit  the
        approved PIP to the Audit Team at  least  ten  (10)  calendar  days
        before the scheduled start of the inventory taking activities.


     3. Ensure the full implementation of the “One-time  cleansing  of  PPE
        accounts” and undertake all other procedures as provided for in COA
        Circular 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and  correct  balances
        of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
   1. Direct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  provide  the  program  of  works
      judiciously and conduct timely procurement proceedings  to  avoid  the
      delayed implementation of the PPAs.


   2. Encourage close coordination among the offices concerned  (MPDO,  MBO,
      MEO and the BAC) to ensure that projects are implemented according  to
      plans and their allotted budget.


   3. As head of the agency, to ensure the optimal utilization  of  the  20%
      development fund in accordance with the updated guidelines  to  attain
      the desired outcomes for the benefit of the constituents of Caraga.


   4. Direct the department heads concerned to prioritize the implementation
      of the 18 remaining projects,  likewise,  the  proposed  developmental
      projects intended for CY 2022 and  warrant  the  completion  of  those
      projects from the prior years’ continuing appropriation.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the MDRRMO shall prepare the monthly Report  of  Sources
and Utilization of MDRRMF to be certified by the  Municipal  Accountant  and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor  on  or  before  the  15th  day
after the end of  each  month  through  the  LDRRMC  and  Local  Development
Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

Moreover,  she  shall  be  cognizant  of  the  pertinent  laws,  rules   and
regulations following the implementation and utilization of MDRRM funds.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

     1.  Direct  the  official/personnel  concerned  to  comply  with   the
        prescribed guidelines set under COA Circular No. 2018-002 in  order
        to insure the properties and supplies of the LGU and to conform  to
        RA No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245; and


     2. Allocate a sufficient budget for the application of  insurance  for
        the properties in the ensuing years.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following courses of action for the Management to:

       1. Ensure that the monthly net home take pay  of  the  employees  and
          officials shall be no less than P5,000.00 as stipulated in Section
          55 of GAA of 2022 to ensure that  government  employees  can  meet
          their families' basic needs and  reduce  their  need  to  take  on
          debts.


       2. Direct those in charge of  the  payroll  preparation  to  exercise
          diligence in monitoring the net take-home pay of its personnel  to
          comply with the requirements of existing law.


       3. Loan repayments are suggested for direct payment  to  the  lending
          institutions.

Recommendations:

We recommended that all purchases of office supplies commonly  used  in  the
LGU’s operations shall be made through the PS-DBM as required by law.

In cases where the office  supplies  from  PS-DBM  are  not  available,  the
certification issued  by  the  PS-DBM  indicating  the  non-availability  of
stocks shall be the basis for the BAC to recommend the  use  of  alternative
modes of procurement subject to the approval of the head  of  the  procuring
entity.

Moreover, the BAC shall explore using online technology  in  obtaining  CNAS
from the PS-DBM to save time and effort in traveling to the Regional  Office
to secure one.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
observe the deadline set in Section 9  of  RA  5447  on  the  remittance  of
provincial shares accruing to the SEF so that the Provincial Government  can
timely utilize the collections to finance various  projects,  programs,  and
activities  beneficial  to  the  constituents  of  the  province  of   Davao
Oriental.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall monitor  and  remind  regularly  the
staff in charge of the timely remittance of  the  RPT  share  to  avoid  the
incurrence of delay.

Recommendations:

We recommended that  members  of  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  and  the  support
personnel shall economically use the  scarce  government  funds.  Also,  the
appropriation for Office of the Sangguniang Bayan and that of the  Municipal
Vice-Mayor  shall  be  confined  only  for  regular  maintenance  and  other
operating expenses of the subject offices.

In addition, we recommended that the Office of the Sangguniang  Bayan  shall
submit the schedule of the activities to  be  conducted  and  shall  furnish
this Office with the ordinances enacted in CY 2022 for  monitoring  purposes
of this Office.

Lastly, submit a written justification to preclude the issuance  of  notices
of suspension/disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation / s:
We recommended the following:
1. For the Municipal Mayor, assign additional manpower to the Accounting
Office whose main function is to focus on the preparation of the 16 Bank
Reconciliation Statements in order to update and prioritize its timely
submission.
58
2. For the Municipal Accountant, effect the necessary adjusting entries once
the supporting documents are already complete in order to establish the
validity and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances in the financial
statements.
3. For the Municipal Treasurer, send a written notice to the payees whose
check became stale to inform the existence of such pursuant to Section 59,
Volume 1 of the NGAS for LGUs.

Recommendation / s:
We recommended the enforcement of the
immediate liquidation of the
outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and
employees concerned and strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-
002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Also, conduct verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and
unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability. A request for the
64
write-off shall be made on dormant receivable accounts by submitting duly certified
relevant documents.

Recommendations / s:
We recommended that the designated GSO shall conduct a complete physical
count on all the properties of the municipality, reconcile the same with the records of the
accounting office and submit the RPCPPE using the prescribed forms thereafter.

Recommendation / s:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall submit immediately the
reported lacking vouchers to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension on these
transactions. Also, responsible offices, as mentioned in Table 1, shall submit financial
and other reports within the prescribed deadlines for a timely review and evaluation of
transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy, otherwise, the penal
provision under Section 122 of PD 1445 shall be applied which states:
“Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents
and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of
payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the
requirements of the Commission.” (Emphasis ours)

observations.managementAction:
Demand letters
were sent by the
Municipal
Accountant to some
of the accountable
officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
succeeding transfers
to barangays shall be
for purposes aligned
with the updated
guidelines on the
utilization of the 20%
development fund.
To facilitate the
monitoring of
implementation of
projects, funds
transferred to
barangays should be
recorded as a debit
to account Due from
Local Government
Units and credited
only upon submission
of supporting
documents proving
its implementation to
facilitate the
monitoring of
implementation of
projects.
Also, direct the
Municipal Planning
and Development
Office to conduct
regular monitoring of
the implementation of
the projects.
observations.managementAction:
Fund transfers to
barangay were
recorded as Due from
Local Government
Units.
observations.reason:
In CY 2019, it was
noted that several
fund transfers
remain
unimplemented at the
barangay level.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that succeeding transfers to barangays shall be for purposes aligned with the updated guidelines on the utilization of the 20% development fund. To facilitate the monitoring of implementation of projects, funds transferred to barangays should be recorded as a debit to account Due from Local Government Units and credited only upon submission of supporting documents proving its implementation. Also, direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to conduct regular monitoring of the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The same practice in 2018 per AOM No. 19-004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall direct the concerned personnel to submit the aforementioned lacking documents. Also, the Accountant shall ensure complete and proper documentation and proper recording of all claims against government funds. Moreover, the acceptance and inspection of all purchases shall be done in accordance with the manual to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management shall submit a written justification on the incurrence of shortfall of appropriation for the payments of loan amortization.

Moreover, the Budget Officer shall ensure that amounts appropriated are enough to cover the requirements for the succeeding payments of loan amortization.
observations.managementAction: Re-computed the amount of loan amortization based on the Memorandum of Agreement with the bank.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the immediate submission of the abovementioned documents to enable us to make a decision in audit. Management should also direct the personnel concerned to ensure complete and timely submission of supporting documents of all contracts executed by the Municipal Government.

We further recommended that the MEO shall submit a written justification on the inconsistencies noted in the supporting documents. Also, he shall regularly monitor the project implementation and take appropriate actions if delays are incurred by the contractor.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No justification was submitted by the MEO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall adhere strictly to the time frame in which the ESWMP be implemented and facilitate the establishment of MRFs in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under Section 32 of RA No. 9003 for the welfare of the entire populace of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Construction materials were on hand, however, no MRFs were constructed.
observations.reason: MRFs were not yet constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall initiate collection of the overpaid amount to the contractor in the total amount of P7,574,368.44. Also, submit justification on other deficiencies noted by COA TAS as mentioned in the attached Technical Evaluation Reports.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No documents were submitted on the action taken to initiate collection of the overpaid amount to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the Inventory and PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No complete inventory count was made for livelihood supplies.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the Invoice and Receipt of Property and the approval from the Sangguniang Bayan on the transfer of properties. Otherwise, prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct Power Supply Systems account balance at year-end.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Same observation in prior years.

observations.recommendation: Submit the necessary lacking documents to avoid suspension/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Lacking documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that copies of perfected contracts and POs are submitted to COA within five working days from the approval of the contract or issuance of the PO.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Same observation in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the validity of the reconciling items and record the same in the books. Henceforth, Bank Reconciliation Statements submitted to the Office of the Auditor should be supported by copies of the Journal Entry Voucher recording reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts for verification. Likewise, debit/credit memo and paid checks shall be submitted immediately to COA.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made to locate the source documents for these reconciling items.
observations.reason: Source documents were not located. The reconciling items cannot be recorded in the books pending verification of its accuracy.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

      We recommended the timely preparation and immediate submission of  the
Bank   Reconciliation   Statements   on   all   bank   accounts    including
paid/cancelled  checks,  debit/credit  memo  and  bank  statements  to  make
possible the verification of the propriety and accuracy of the  Cash-in-Bank
balances on the financial statements during the year.


      Also, make the necessary adjusting entries for the noted errors in the
books of the agency.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the enforcement of the  immediate  liquidation  of  the
outstanding cash  advances  thru  the  issuance  of  letters  of  demand  to
officials and employees concerned and strict compliance with the  provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and  liquidation  of
cash advances.


      Also,  conduct  verification  and  analysis  of   the   existence   of
receivables and unliquidated  cash  advances  to  determine  the  extent  of
collectability. A request  for  the  write-off  shall  be  made  on  dormant
receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that  the  designated  GSO  shall  conduct  a  complete
physical count on all the properties  of  the  municipality,  reconcile  the
same with the records of the accounting office and submit the  RPCPPE  using
the prescribed format, thereafter.


      We further recommended the keeping and updating of  ledger  cards  and
property  cards  to  facilitate  reconciliation  of   physical   count   and
accounting/property records.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall cause the return of
the unutilized balance  to  the  SA,  unless  otherwise  the  Memorandum  of
Agreement so provides.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Mayor shall prioritize the construction of the
sanitary landfill and discontinue  the  use  of  the  open  dump.  Moreover,
management shall strictly adhere to the provisions of  the  R.A.  No.  9003.
Moreover,  consider  the  issuance  of  termination  of  contract  and   the
blacklisting of contractor, without prejudice to  other  courses  of  action
available to the agency.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Accounting Office shall maintain  the  special
accounts  in  the  General  Fund  in  accordance  with  the   abovementioned
guidelines.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  submit   the
liquidation reports for the above-mentioned cash  advances.  Human  Resource
Resources and Management Officer and the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  also
require the personnel under job  orders  the  submission  of  accomplishment
report that will serve as basis for the claim for payment of wages.

We recommended the preparation and immediate submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts including paid/cancelled checks, debit / credit memo and bank statements to make possible the verification of the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances on the financial statements during the year.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reconciliation was made by the Municipal Accountant between the bank and book balances for CYs 2015 to 2018 for the Philippine Veterans Bank account of 022-0068xx-xxx and September to December 2018 for the Development Bank of the Philippines account 00-0-05938-xxx-x.
observations.reason: 14 bank accounts were not yet reconciled by the Municipal Accountant. Also, an unreconciled balance of 7,611,253.88 exists for PVB account of 022-0068xx-xxx.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the enforcement of the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Also, conduct verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine the extent of collectability. A request for the write-off shall be made on dormant receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant to some of the accountable officers.
observations.reason: Not all AOs were sent demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall record the disallowances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 in the account Receivables-Disallowances/Charges.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the disallowances in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall effect the necessary adjustments to properly reflect the unutilized balance of the DRRMF and to closely monitor and review the recording of transactions in the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant on the transfers to/from.
observations.reason: Adjusting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant in total amount of P15,495,925.00 charged to the account Prior Year's Adjustments in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall strengthen the workforce of the MDRRM office by appropriating the funds needed for the mandatory positions. If the financial capability of the agency permits so, hire additional personnel other than those required by the JMC to ensure proper functioning of the office in the implementation and monitoring of PPAs, thereby reducing disaster risks and life-threatening damages to the entire populace of Caraga. Also, submit the required reports in accordance with the established laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Funds were appropriated for MDRRMO III and MDRRM Assistant II, however, only MDRRMO III was filled-up.
observations.reason:
The LDRRM office is manned by an Action Officer, 2 personnel for clerical works and driver, hence without sufficient workforce.

Reports on the Sources and Utilization were not in the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LSB shall be advised to align the PPAs charged against SEF with the enumerated allowable expenses of the applicable provisions.

Likewise, expedite the utilization of the fund for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended to strictly follow the required amount of the employer premium contribution as authorized under RA No. 9679.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the required amount of the employer premium contribution as authorized under RA No. 9679.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall submit immediately the reported lacking vouchers to preclude issuance of Notices of Suspension. Also, personnel concerned shall strictly observe and submit the required financial and other reports within the prescribed deadlines to effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy, otherwise, the penal provision under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 shall be applied.
observations.managementAction: Office of the Municipal Accountant constantly reminds the Office of the Municipal Treasurer on the submission of vouchers. Overtime services were rendered by the personnel-in-charge, hence, rate of submission of vouchers has improved.
observations.reason: Lacking vouchers of prior years were partially submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the designated GSO shall conduct physical count on all properties of the municipality, reconcile the same with the records of the accounting office and submit the RPCPPE thereafter.

b. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties.

c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the final undetermined balance which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

d. Maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of actual physical count and the recorded balances in the accounting/property records.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted by the designated GSO as at December 31, 2018 which only included physical count of PPE purchased in the current year.
observations.reason: RPCPPE as at December 31, 2018 did not include PPE of 2017 and prior years, hence, an unreconciled balance of P88,644,978.91.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the accountant or her authorized representative shall submit the cash advance vouchers with covering transmittal letters and the supporting documents within 7 days after release of check.

Also, the accountant shall ensure that total cash advances granted to disbursing officers shall be limited only to the maximum cash accountability of the approved amount of bond.
observations.managementAction:
The accountant complied with audit recommendation.

The accountant shall ensure that total cash advances granted to disbursing officers shall be limited only to the maximum cash accountability of the approved amount of bond.
observations.reason: Cash advances granted to disbursing officer were not limited to the maximum cash accountability of the approved amount of bond.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall closely monitor the utilization of funds transferred from national government agencies to cause the timely implementation of PPAs. Moreover, immediately cause the return of the unutilized balance to the SA, unless otherwise the Memorandum of Agreement so provides.
observations.managementAction: Partial return of unutilized amounts.
observations.reason: Existence of long outstanding transfers from NGAs still exist as communicated in AOM Nos. 19-002 and 19-003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE shall ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% development fund in accordance with the updated guidelines to attain the desired outcomes for the benefit of the constituents of Caraga.
observations.managementAction: Assessment on the viability of implementation of CY 2017 and prior years development fund projects resulted to re-appropriation of the fund to new development projects. Partial implementation was made on such projects and to the existing unimplemented projects.
observations.reason: Partial implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to update, as necessary, the LDRRMFIP to include the immediately preceding year's LDRRMF balance. Also, the MDRRM office shall ensure optimal utilization of the available LDDRMF for disaster risk management activities and purchase of disaster rescue and response equipment to ensure readiness in mitigating the effect of any disaster that may happen.
observations.managementAction: Resolution No. 01-2018 dated March 26, 2018 was issued by the MDRRM Council, as adopted and approved by the local sanggunian through resolution No. 103 dated September 12, 2018, for the updated LDRRMIP to reprogram the said fund for new expenditure items.
observations.reason: LDRRMIP was adopted and approved, however, utilization rate has not improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall expedite the utilization of the fund throughout the year to ensure that the planned PPAs contribute to the prevention of juvenile delinquency and promotion of the rights and welfare of children.
observations.managementAction: Called the attention of the focal person to address the concern on the timely implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: Expedite the implementation of intended PPAs for which the funds were received and return to source agencies the residual amounts of completed projects. Otherwise, revert the same to the General Fund of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partial return of unutilized amounts.
observations.reason: Existence of long outstanding transfers from NGAs still exist as communicated in AOM Nos. 19-002 and 19-003.

observations.recommendation: Effect the necessary revisions without prejudice to the rules and regulations of DOLE RO XI and expedite the implementation thereof. We also recommended the submission of documents pertaining to the project.
observations.managementAction: The amount was returned to the source agency on January 2018 due to the non-accreditation of intended people's organization.

observations.recommendation: Limit the charges to LGDF to the implementation of development projects and refrain from use of the same for administrative and regular operating expenses.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Budgeted amounts in CY 2018 were in accordance with guidelines.

observations.recommendation: Allocate funds from the General Fund for the administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office and ensure that charges to the LDRRM fund are limited only to PPAs in support of disaster risk reduction and management activities.
observations.managementAction: Budget was provided for the current operating expenditures of the office.
observations.reason: The same observation was made in CY 2017. as prescribed.

observations.recommendation: Submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day of the succeeding month to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was submitted.
observations.reason: Reports submitted were not the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that disbursements are properly recorded to prevent inaccurate and misleading information.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Municipal School Board to utilize the SEF for programs/projects/activities which are aligned to enumerated priority expenses of the applicable provisions and not to restrict mainly on sports activities. We also recommended strict adherence to prescribed deadlines on submission of reports and schedules.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: Submit the lacking supporting documents and the duly accomplished Acceptance and Inspection Report.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the noted supporting documents.
observations.reason: As indicated in AAR 2016, some of the lacking documents were submitted by BAC.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.

b. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties.

c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the final undetermined balance which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

d. Maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of physical count and accounting/property records.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted by the designated GSO as at December 31, 2018 which only included physical count of PPE purchased in the current year.
observations.reason: RPCPPE as at December 31, 2018 did not include PPE of 2017 and prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended further that any adjustments/corrections found in the course of reconciliation shall be taken up in the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reconciliation was made by the Municipal Accountant between the bank and book balances for CYs 2015 to 2018 for the Philippine Veterans Bank account of 022-0068xx-xxx and September to December 2018 for the Development Bank of the Philippines account 00-0-05938-xxx-x.
observations.reason: 14 bank accounts were not yet reconciled by the Municipal Accountant. Also, an unreconciled balance of 7,611,253.88 exists for PVB account of 022-0068xx-xxx.

observations.recommendation:
Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials and employees concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Conduct regular and periodic verification and analysis of the existence of receivables and unliquidated cash advances to determine extent of collectability. Request for the write-off of dormant accounts and submit the certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent by the Accountant to some of the accountable officers. However, there was no substantial movement of balances on the Other Receivables account.
observations.reason: Not all AOs were sent demand letters. Partial liquidation was made on cash advances from 2014 to 2017. However, no liquidation reports were submitted to this office.

observations.recommendation: Direct the personnel concerned to strictly observe and submit the required financial and other reports within the prescribed deadlines. Require the immediate submission of reported lacking vouchers to preclude issuance of Notices of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the vouchers incurring minimal days of delay.
observations.reason: Bulk submission of DVs in the last quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: The Treasurer shall reconcile his cashbook balance with the collection daily and also with that of the ledger balance maintained by the Accountant at least once in every quarter.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted effort in locating the documents as basis for the adjustments.
observations.reason: Documents that would allow the reconciliation of variances from prior years including that of previous accountable officers could not be located hence, reconciliation of the abovementioned variances could not be done.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all bank accounts including paid/cancelled checks, debit/credit memo and bank statements, to make possible the verification of the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank balances on the financial statements during the year and to effect the necessary corrections/adjustments found in the course of reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reconciliation was made by the Municipal Accountant between the bank and book balances for CYs 2015 to 2018 for the Philippine Veterans Bank account of 022-0068xx-xxx and September to December 2018 for the Development Bank of the Philippines account 00-0-05938-xxx-x. Monthly snapshots were requested from the bank in lieu of delayed forwarding of bank statements. Additional personnel were hired for the preparation of the BRS. Moreover, the agency is on the process of locating source documents to validate previous BRS.
observations.reason: 14 bank accounts were not yet reconciled by the Municipal Accountant. Also, an unreconciled balance of 7,611,253.88 exists for PVB account of 022-0068xx-xxx.

observations.recommendation: We require the accountable officer to settle/liquidate immediately the above-mentioned cash advances and to observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation thereof. The agency is also reminded that failure to settle the outstanding accounts shall be ground for administrative and criminal measures provided in COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent by the Accountant to some of the accountable officers. However, there was no substantial movement of balances on the Other Receivables account.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to monitor the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

a. Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment with the corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.

b. Reconcile the result thereof with available accounting records to determine unrecorded properties.

c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the final undetermined balance which shall be our basis for appropriate opinion and further action.

d. Maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of physical count and accounting/property records.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted by the designated GSO as at December 31, 2018 which only included physical count of PPE purchased in the current year.
observations.reason: RPCPPE as at December 31, 2018 did not include PPE of 2017 and prior years.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      We recommended the preparation and immediate submission  of  the  Bank
Reconciliation Statements on  all  bank  accounts  including  paid/cancelled
checks,  debit/credit  memo  and  bank  statements  to  make  possible   the
verification of the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank  balances  on
the financial statements during the year.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the enforcement of the  immediate  liquidation  of  the
outstanding cash  advances  thru  the  issuance  of  letters  of  demand  to
officials and employees concerned and strict compliance with the  provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and  liquidation  of
cash advances. Also, conduct verification and analysis of the  existence  of
receivables and unliquidated  cash  advances  to  determine  the  extent  of
collectability. A request  for  the  write-off  shall  be  made  on  dormant
receivable accounts by submitting duly certified relevant documents.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  record   the
disallowances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006  in  the  account
Receivables- Disallowances/Charges.

Recommendations:


      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  effect   the
necessary adjustments to properly reflect  the  unutilized  balance  of  the
DRRMF and to closely monitor and review the  recording  of  transactions  in
the books of the agency.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that succeeding transfers to  barangays  shall  be  for
purposes aligned with the updated guidelines on the utilization of  the  20%
development  fund.  To  facilitate  the  monitoring  of  implementation   of
projects, funds transferred to barangays should be recorded as  a  debit  to
account Due from Local Government Units and credited  only  upon  submission
of  supporting  documents  proving  its  implementation.  Also,  direct  the
Municipal Planning and Development Office to conduct regular  monitoring  of
the implementation of the projects.

Recommendations:


      We  recommended  the  immediate  submission  of   the   abovementioned
documents to enable us to make a decision in audit. Management  should  also
direct the personnel concerned to ensure complete and timely  submission  of
supporting documents of all contracts executed by the Municipal Government.


      We  further  recommended  that  the  MEO  shall   submit   a   written
justification on the inconsistencies  noted  in  the  supporting  documents.
Also, he  shall  regularly  monitor  the  project  implementation  and  take
appropriate actions if delays are incurred by the contractor.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management shall adhere strictly to the time frame
in which the ESWMP be implemented and facilitate the establishment  of  MRFs
in every barangay or cluster of barangays as required under  Section  32  of
RA No. 9003 for the welfare of the entire populace of the municipality.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the  LSB  shall  be  advised  to  align  the  PPAs
charged  against  SEF  with  the  enumerated  allowable  expenses   of   the
abovementioned applicable provisions. Likewise, expedite the utilization  of
the fund for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that the designated GSO shall conduct physical count on
all the properties of the municipality, reconcile the same with the  records
of the accounting office and submit the RPCPPE thereafter.


      We have further recommended the keeping and updating of  ledger  cards
and property cards  to  facilitate  reconciliation  of  physical  count  and
accounting/property records.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the accountant or  her  authorized  representative
shall submit the cash advance vouchers  with  covering  transmittal  letters
and the supporting documents within 7 days after  release  of  check.  Also,
the accountant shall ensure that total cash advances granted  to  disbursing
officers shall be limited only to the maximum  cash  accountability  of  the
approved amount of bond.

Recommendations:


      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall closely monitor the
utilization of funds transferred from national government agencies to  cause
the timely implementation of PPAs. Moreover, immediately  cause  the  return
of the unutilized balance to the SA,  unless  otherwise  the  Memorandum  of
Agreement so provides.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the LCE shall ensure the  optimal  utilization  of
the 20% development fund  in  accordance  with  the  updated  guidelines  to
attain the desired outcomes for the benefit of the constituents of Caraga.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to update, as necessary, the LDRRMFIP to  include  the
immediately preceding year's LDRRMF balance. Also, the MDRRM  office  shall
ensure optimal utilization  of  the  available  LDDRMF  for  disaster  risk
management  activities  and  purchase  of  disaster  rescue  and   response
equipment to ensure readiness in mitigating the effect of any disaster that
may happen.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that management shall expedite the utilization  of  the
fund throughout the year to ensure that the planned PPAs contribute  to  the
prevention of juvenile delinquency and promotion of the rights  and welfare
of children.

Recommendations:


      We recommended  that  management  shall  initiate  collection  of  the
overpaid amount to the contractor in  the  total  amount  of  P7,574,368.44.
Also, submit justification  on  other  deficiencies  noted  by  COA  TAS  as
mentioned in the attached Technical Evaluation Reports.

Recommendations:

      Expedite the implementation of intended PPAs for which the funds  were
received and return to source agencies the  residual  amounts  of  completed
projects.  Otherwise,  revert  the  same  to  the  General   Fund   of   the
municipality.

Recommendation:

       Make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct  balances
of the Inventory and PPE accounts.

Recommendations:

      Effect the necessary revisions without  prejudice  to  the  rules  and
regulations of DOLE RO XI and expedite the implementation thereof.  We  also
recommended the submission of documents pertaining to the project.

Recommendation:

      Limit the  charges  to  LGDF  to  the  implementation  of  development
projects and refrain from use of the same  for  administrative  and  regular
operating expenses.

Recommendation:

      Submit immediately  the  Invoice  and  Receipt  of  Property  and  the
approval  from  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  on  the  transfer  of   properties.
Otherwise, prepare the necessary journal  entries  to  reflect  the  correct
Power Supply Systems account balance at year-end.

Recommendations:

     a. Allocate  funds  from  the  General  Fund  for  the  administrative
        expenses of the MDRRM Office and ensure that charges to  the  LDRRM
        fund are limited only to PPAs in support of disaster risk reduction
        and management activities.
     b.   Submit    the    necessary    lacking    documents    to    avoid
        suspension/disallowance.
     c. Submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of  LDRRMF  on
        or before the 15th day of the succeeding month to the Office of the
        Auditor.
     d.  Ensure  that  disbursements  are  properly  recorded  to   prevent
        inaccurate and misleading information.

Recommendations:

       Advise  the  Municipal  School  Board  to   utilize   the   SEF   for
programs/projects/activities  which  are  aligned  to  enumerated   priority
expenses of abovementioned applicable provisions and not to restrict  mainly
on sports activities. We also recommended  strict  adherence  to  prescribed
deadlines on submission of reports and schedules.

Recommendations:

     a. Ensure that copies of perfected contracts and POs are submitted  to
        COA within five working days from the approval of the  contract  or
        issuance of the PO.
     b. Submit the lacking supporting documents and the  duly  accomplished
        Acceptance and Inspection Report.

Recommendations:

      In view of the above observations, we reiterate  our  previous  years’
recommendations, to wit:


    a. Direct the inventory/property committee to conduct an  inventory  of
       all the properties of the Municipality and prepare the Report on the
       Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment  (RPCPPE)  with  the
       corresponding remarks on the condition of the listed equipment.
    b. Reconcile the result thereof with available  accounting  records  to
       determine unrecorded properties. Consider PPE that may  be  included
       in the undetermined forwarded balance.
    c. Submit a report on the result of reconciliation including the  final
       undetermined balance  which  shall  be  our  basis  for  appropriate
       opinion and further action.
    d.  Maintain  PPE  Ledger  Cards  and  Property  Cards  to   facilitate
       reconciliation of actual physical count and the recorded balances in
       the accounting/property records.

Recommendations:


      We recommended the preparation and immediate submission  of  the  Bank
Reconciliation Statements on  all  bank  accounts  including  paid/cancelled
checks,  debit/credit  memo  and  bank  statements  to  make  possible   the
verification of the propriety and accuracy of the Cash-in-Bank  balances  on
the financial statements during the year. We recommended  further  that  any
adjustments/corrections found in  the  course  of  reconciliation  shall  be
taken up in the books of the agency.

Recommendations:

     a. Enforce the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash  advances
        thru the issuance of letters of demand to officials  and  employees
        concerned and strictly comply with the provisions of  COA  Circular
        97-002  in  the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances.
     b. Conduct regular and  periodic  verification  and  analysis  of  the
        existence  of  receivables  and  unliquidated  cash   advances   to
        determine extent of collectability.
     c. Request for the  write-off  of  dormant  accounts  and  submit  the
        certified  relevant  documents  validating  the  existence  of  the
        conditions.

Recommendation:

      Reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make  the  necessary  correcting
entries. Moreover, the  Municipal  Accountant  should  closely  monitor  and
review the recording of transactions in the books of the agency.

Recommendations:

      Direct the personnel concerned to  strictly  observe  and  submit  the
required financial and other reports  within  the  prescribed  deadlines  to
effect timely review and evaluation of transactions as to  their  propriety,
completeness and  accuracy,  otherwise, the penal provision under Section  122
of PD 1445 shall be applied.
      Require the immediate submission of the reported lacking  vouchers  to
preclude issuance of Notices of Suspension on these transactions.
